title: pteridium caudatum subsp. yarrabense
intro: Pteridium caudatum subsp. yarrabense, also known as Yarra fern or Mountain fern, is a subspecies of the fern plant Pteridium caudatum, which is native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing swelling and fighting off infections. The health benefits of Pteridium caudatum subsp. yarrabense include its ability to soothe digestive issues, lower blood pressure, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or applying them directly to the affected area.
